rationale: The citing note says the 2010 paper "extends social comparison research in marketing from consumer settings to employee incentive contract design," and the cited 2005 paper is exactly a formal + experimental consumer model of social effects (uniqueness/conformity) in utility and market outcomes. That makes the cited work a plausible conceptual/theory source rather than a clearly incidental mention, even though the matched evidence here is only DOI-level metadata.
